Package org.apache.juneau.commons.svl

Class TypedFunction

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.TypedFunction
All Implemented Interfaces:
VarFunction
Direct Known Subclasses:
ArithmeticFunctions.Abs, ArithmeticFunctions.Add, ArithmeticFunctions.Divide, ArithmeticFunctions.Max, ArithmeticFunctions.Min, ArithmeticFunctions.Modulo, ArithmeticFunctions.Multiply, ArithmeticFunctions.Subtract, BooleanFunctions.And, BooleanFunctions.Eq, BooleanFunctions.Gt, BooleanFunctions.Gte, BooleanFunctions.Lt, BooleanFunctions.Lte, BooleanFunctions.Neq, BooleanFunctions.Not, BooleanFunctions.Or, BooleanFunctions.Xor, ConditionalFunctions.Coalesce, ConditionalFunctions.If, ConditionalFunctions.NotEmpty, ConditionalFunctions.Switch, DateFunctions.FormatDate, DateFunctions.Now, DateFunctions.ParseDate, EncodingFunctions.Base64Decode, EncodingFunctions.Base64Encode, EncodingFunctions.HtmlEscape, EncodingFunctions.HtmlUnescape, EncodingFunctions.UrlDecode, EncodingFunctions.UrlEncode, JsonFunctions.Get, JsonFunctions.JsonPath, JsonFunctions.Keys, JsonFunctions.Size, JsonFunctions.Values, RandomFunctions.Rand, RandomFunctions.RandChoice, RandomFunctions.RandInt, RandomFunctions.RandLong, RandomFunctions.RandString, RandomFunctions.Uuid, RegexFunctions.Extract, RegexFunctions.Match, RegexFunctions.ReplaceRegex, StringFunctions.Concat, StringFunctions.Contains, StringFunctions.EndsWith, StringFunctions.Format, StringFunctions.Join, StringFunctions.Len, StringFunctions.Lower, StringFunctions.PathToken, StringFunctions.Repeat, StringFunctions.Replace, StringFunctions.Reverse, StringFunctions.Split, StringFunctions.StartsWith, StringFunctions.StripLeading, StringFunctions.StripSlashes, StringFunctions.StripTrailing, StringFunctions.Substring, StringFunctions.Trim, StringFunctions.Upper, TypeConversionFunctions.ToBool, TypeConversionFunctions.ToDouble, TypeConversionFunctions.ToInt, TypeConversionFunctions.ToLong
public abstract class TypedFunction extends Object implements VarFunction
Reflection-based base class for VarFunction implementations that prefer typed argument signatures over manual List<Object> unpacking.

Subclasses declare one or more public invoke(...) methods whose parameter lists mirror the function's expected arguments. Per-arg target types and arities are derived once at construction via reflection; per-call dispatch picks the matching overload by argument count and runs one reflective invoke + N ArgCoercer coercions.

Method-resolution rules:
  • The subclass declares one or more public methods named invoke. Each must have a distinct fixed-arity (or be the single variadic overload — see below).
  • The return type must be String (or a subtype convertible via Object.toString()).
  • Parameter types may be any of: String, int, long, double, boolean (or their wrappers), String[], or Object. The final slot may be String[] for variadic functions.
  • If the subclass needs the session (e.g. to look up beans), declare VarResolverSession as the first parameter.
  • At most one variadic overload (final String[] slot) per subclass; combined with fixed-arity overloads as long as the arity counts don't overlap.
Example — multi-arity:

public class Substring extends TypedFunction { @Override public String name() { return "substring"; } public String invoke(String s, int start) { return s.substring(start); } public String invoke(String s, int start, int end) { return s.substring(start, end); } }

  • Constructor Details

    • TypedFunction

      protected TypedFunction()

  • Method Details

    • minArity

      public final int minArity()
      Description copied from interface: VarFunction
      Minimum argument count this function accepts.
      Specified by:
      minArity in interface VarFunction
      Returns:
      The minimum number of args. 0 means the function may be called with no args. Returns -1 for "any number of args" — equivalent to declaring minArity == 0 && maxArity == Integer.MAX_VALUE.

    • maxArity

      public final int maxArity()
      Description copied from interface: VarFunction
      Maximum argument count this function accepts.
      Specified by:
      maxArity in interface VarFunction
      Returns:
      The maximum number of args, or Integer.MAX_VALUE for unbounded variadic functions.

    • invoke

      public final String invoke(VarResolverSession session, List<Object> args)
      Description copied from interface: VarFunction
      Invokes this function with already-resolved, type-coerced arguments.

      The runtime resolves each #{...} arg template against the session, then coerces the resolved string to the target Java type via ArgCoercer. The resulting List<Object> is passed in here.

      Specified by:
      invoke in interface VarFunction
      Parameters:
      session - The current resolver session. Must not be null.
      args - The coerced arguments. Size is in [minArity(), maxArity()]. Each element is the type the function expects per its signature; for variadic / Object-typed slots, elements remain String.
      Returns:
      The function's result as a String. May be empty but should not be null (the runtime treats null as the empty string for SVL output).
